facts.text: Bay Lake takes its name from a lake east of the Magic Kingdom, the original water body that inspired Disney’s resort.
The city’s borders encompass the entire Walt Disney World Resort, making it the only municipality that surrounds the famed theme parks.
All long‑term residents live in a mobile‑home park on Bay Court, a street that was a dirt road until 1987.
Bay Lake was incorporated inside the Reedy Creek Improvement District, a special taxing district that holds most of the city’s powers.
The area experiences hot, humid summers and generally mild winters, a typical Gulf‑Coast climate that keeps visitors comfortable most of the year.
Disney’s original master plan for the site envisioned a futuristic living area—a “community of the future”—though only some ideas survived into EPCOT.
